external_narratives.summary: Crash site investigation for Hudson N7294 of 220 Squadron at Easby Moor on 11 February 1940. Three Hudsons took off from Thornaby for an operational flight to the Heligoland area; the lead aircraft's windscreen iced up and it struck Easby Moor near the Captain Cook Monument.
key_details: Part of three-aircraft formation to Heligoland
Crashed near Captain Cook Monument on Easby Moor
Flew through a stone wall on impact

external_narratives.summary: Record of Hudson N7294 crash at Easby Moor, Great Ayton on 11 February 1940. Three crew killed; LAC Atholl Barker survived with injuries and later returned to service with 220 Squadron.
key_details: F/O Tom Parker (pilot) age 21, buried Thornaby
Sgt Harold Bleksley age 24, buried Bristol
Cpl Norman Drury age 23, buried North Walsham
LAC Barker survived, returned to 220 Squadron

external_narratives.summary: Accident record for Lockheed Hudson Mk I N7294 on 11 February 1940. Documents the crash circumstances including icing conditions and impact location on Easby Moor.
key_details: One of earliest WW2 crash sites in North Yorkshire

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: Photon OSM — Easby Moor named entry at 54.482 -1.092
Postcodes.io — Easby TS9 (Great Ayton hamlet) at 54.470 -1.111
location_confidence.verification_notes: INACCURATE. Previous coords (53.986, -1.436) ~55km south near Harrogate/Ripon — completely wrong. Easby Moor is above Great Ayton in Cleveland Hills. Photon has named Easby Moor node at 54.482 -1.092.
